findings.category: A01: Broken Access Control / Path Traversal
findings.title: Path traversal risk in taste-profile.md write path
findings.description: The tech-spec instructs design-retrospective to write to `.design-system/taste-profile.md` using a fixed path. However, if a project category name or other user-influenced input were ever interpolated into the path (e.g., dynamic subdirectories), path traversal could occur. Currently the spec uses a hardcoded fixed path which is safe. The existing design-generate skill already validates file names with `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` for pages. The same validation pattern should be explicitly required for any future file names derived from user input in the taste-profile or experience flows.
findings.impact: Low risk currently since the path is fixed. If future iterations add dynamic naming, unvalidated input could write outside `.design-system/`.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit instruction in the tech-spec that any file names derived from user input (e.g., project category names used as section headers) must be validated against `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` before use in file paths. The current fixed-path approach is safe and should be preserved.

findings.title: No file size limit on append-only designer-experience.md
findings.description: The designer-experience.md file uses append-only writes across all projects over time. There is no defined size cap or rotation strategy. Over months or years, this file could grow unboundedly, leading to performance degradation (large file reads consuming agent context budget) or potential denial of service for the agent's context window.
findings.impact: The file gradually consumes more of the agent's token budget when read. At extreme sizes it could crowd out useful context. Not a traditional security vulnerability but a design resilience issue.
findings.recommendation: Add a guideline in the tech-spec: if designer-experience.md exceeds a threshold (e.g., 500 lines or 5000 words), the design-retrospective skill should archive older entries or summarize them. Alternatively, document that the agent reads only the matching category section, which naturally limits read scope. The current design of reading only one category section partially mitigates this.

findings.title: Corrupted file recovery could lose data silently
findings.description: The user-spec states that a corrupted taste-profile.md should be recreated with a warning. However, the tech-spec does not define what constitutes 'corrupted' (malformed markdown? empty file? binary content?) nor does it specify whether a backup of the corrupted file should be kept before overwriting. Silent data loss on recreation could discard user preferences accumulated over multiple sessions.
findings.location: Section: Acceptance Criteria, edge case handling for corrupted taste-profile
findings.impact: If a partially valid taste-profile is incorrectly classified as corrupted, all accumulated preferences are lost without backup. Low severity since this is a local project file, but it affects user experience.
findings.recommendation: Define corruption criteria explicitly: (1) file is empty, (2) file lacks required section headers, (3) file contains non-text content. Before overwriting, rename the corrupted file to `taste-profile.md.bak` with a timestamp. This is already partially addressed by the 'recreate with warning' instruction but should be more specific.

findings.title: No audit trail for cross-project experience writes
findings.description: When design-retrospective appends to the global designer-experience.md, there is no mechanism to track which project wrote which entry beyond the content of the entry itself. If entries are malformed or incorrect, there is no way to trace them back to their source project for cleanup.
findings.location: Section: Architecture, Write path to designer-experience.md
findings.impact: Difficult to debug or clean up incorrect cross-project experience entries. Low severity since this is a local markdown-based system with no multi-user access.
findings.recommendation: Ensure each appended entry includes: project name, date, and the category it was filed under. The data model in the tech-spec already suggests dated entries, which partially addresses this. Consider adding project name as a mandatory field in each entry.

findings.title: No code injection vector through markdown files - confirmed safe
findings.description: The architecture is entirely markdown-based with no executable code. SKILL.md files contain instructions for an AI agent, not executable scripts. Markdown content in taste-profile.md and designer-experience.md is read as text context, not parsed or executed. There is no deserialization, no eval(), no template engine processing user-written markdown into executable output. The only executable-adjacent content is the HTML generation in design-generate, which already uses a template with CSP meta tags and validates file names. This finding is informational, confirming the absence of code injection risk.
findings.impact: None - this is a positive finding confirming the design is safe against code injection through markdown files.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. The markdown-only architecture inherently avoids code injection. Continue the pattern of not interpreting user-written markdown as executable content.

notes.hardcoded_secrets: No hardcoded secrets detected. The architecture is markdown-only with no API keys, tokens, passwords, or connection strings.

owasp_coverage.A01_broken_access_control: Reviewed. Fixed file paths mitigate path traversal. File name validation pattern exists in design-generate (^[a-z0-9-]+$) and should be carried forward.
owasp_coverage.A02_cryptographic_failures: Not applicable. No cryptographic operations in this feature.
owasp_coverage.A03_injection: Reviewed. No SQL, NoSQL, OS command, or XSS vectors. Markdown is read as text, not executed. HTML generation uses CSP meta tags.
owasp_coverage.A04_insecure_design: Two minor findings: unbounded append-only file growth and underspecified corruption handling.
owasp_coverage.A05_security_misconfiguration: Not applicable. No servers, CORS, or security headers to configure (beyond existing CSP in HTML templates).
owasp_coverage.A06_vulnerable_components: Not applicable. No new dependencies added.
owasp_coverage.A07_auth_failures: Not applicable. No authentication in this feature.
owasp_coverage.A08_software_data_integrity: Reviewed. No deserialization. Markdown files are read as plain text.
owasp_coverage.A09_logging_monitoring: One low finding about audit trail for cross-project writes.
owasp_coverage.A10_ssrf: Not applicable. No network requests in this feature.

notes.information_leakage_assessment: The designer-experience.md file is global and shared across projects. It stores generalized design learnings (color preferences, layout patterns) organized by project category. This does NOT contain sensitive business data, credentials, or PII. The content is aesthetic preferences and design patterns, which are non-sensitive. Cross-project information leakage risk is minimal and acceptable by design.
notes.relative_path_assessment: SKILL.md files reference shared resources via relative paths like `../../shared/design-references/`. These are fixed paths within the repository structure, not user-controlled. The tech-spec includes verification steps (bash test -e) to confirm all reference links resolve. No path traversal risk from relative references in SKILL.md files.
